voice: Warm, direct, hopeful without being saccharine. Q Youth NZ speaks to LGBTQIA+/Takatāpui rangatahi (11-30) and their whānau in Te Tau Ihu / the top of the South Island.
language: NZ English spelling. Te reo Māori terms used naturally (rangatahi, whānau, takatāpui, kōrero) without translation footnotes.
rules: Privacy first: never include names, identifying details, or stories of real young people.
Never speculate about anyone's identity; never 'out' anyone, including public figures, beyond their own public statements.
Ground claims in named, evidence-based sources; cite the organisation or study in-text.
Acknowledge difficulty before offering hope — no toxic positivity, no hollow affirmations.
Age-appropriate for readers as young as 11; plain language, short paragraphs.
Signpost real support: Q Youth contact page, OUTLine NZ 0800 688 5463, Youthline 0800 376 633.
No emojis. Paragraphs separated by blank lines.
structure: 400-700 words. Hook, why it matters here in Te Tau Ihu, what the evidence says, practical steps, where to get support.
